commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor proof and maintainer acceptance of the strict loopback compatibility tradeoff, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression found; the loopback change reduces accidental non-loopback exposure and the PR does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, or package resolution. commands.review_summary:
The PR makes loopback binding strict, adds PID-aware suppression for gateway-owned port hints, and covers the behavior with focused gateway, status, and port-format tests.

PR surface: Source +43, Tests +66. Total +109 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source inspection gives a clear path on current main: loopback bind can fall back to 0.0.0.0 and command-only node listeners can produce unknown/multiple-listener hints, but I did not run the live sandbox status command in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor proof and maintainer acceptance of the strict loopback compatibility tradeoff, not an automated code repair.
